title: Cleanup CIDFile on podman-remote run --rm command
body:
Currently the CIDFile is not removed with podman --remote run --rm if the client and server are on different machines.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED] i
There is currently a test for this that does not fail because the client and server are on the same machine.

If we run these tests on a MAC or Windows platform, they would start failing.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/19420

```release-note
CID Files on remote clients will now be removed when container is removed
```

comments.body: I think we can do better, but perhaps not perfectly.  If we are removing the container on the client side we can remove the cidfile.

> I think we can do better, but perhaps not perfectly. If we are removing the container on the client side we can remove the cidfile.

Agreed, it's an improvement.  Should we document the shortcoming of `--rm -d` not cleaning up?

Hi,

Thanks for fixing this so quickly !

On the code side, the diff seems reasonable, but I barely know the podman code base so I don't think my opinion matters much here ;)

I acknowledge the documented limitation that it can only work in attached mode, and for the use cases I have seen in our company the attached mode is much more used than the detached one. However I wonder if you consider eventually to make this work in detached mode as well (with what I suggested in the issue: fork/exec the podman-remote client in the background, and use the API for "podman wait" to be notified upon container exit) ?

More generally, I know you advertise the fact that podman doesn't use the client/server scheme by default as a good point in comparison to docker (and it's indeed a good point for performances and security). However on this point I actually like the fact that docker always runs in client/server mode, because for us the `--remote` is really very common for CI builds (the most common case being a docker/podman client running inside a container and connect to a docker/podman socket with a server running outside the container, and now with multi-arch build we tend to have more and more "remote" builds on foreign architecture machines as well). There are some other feature that docker implements in remote mode that podman doesn't (yet), is it a goal to try to match these missing remote features ? One that come to my mind immediately and which I would also need is for example this one https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15922.

Cheers,
Romain

Reading the diff once again, this seems to only add new unlink syscall on the client machine, however it doesn't seem the remove the unlink syscall on the server machine (if the container was created from the remote API). In the issue in the last example I showed that if a file exists on the server machine, using the exact same path as cidfile on the client machine, then the server file is being wrongly deleted.

Yes I have done nothing on the server side.  I am not sure how to determine on the server side at that place in the code, that you are on the server.

As far as docker is concerned, does it remove the cidfile automatically on container exit?

> As far as docker is concerned, does it remove the cidfile automatically on container exit?

I thought it did, but my empirical test just now shows it doesn't remove cidfile, no matter if client/server are on the same machine, or if `--detach` is used or not:

```
rgeissler@ncearmdev002:~> docker info|grep Version
 Version:    24.0.2
    Version:  v0.10.5
    Version:  v2.18.1
 Server Version: 24.0.2
 Cgroup Version: 1
 Kernel Version: 4.18.0-477.13.1.el8_8.aarch64
 
 rgeissler@ncearmdev002:~> rm -f /tmp/remove-cidfile-test && docker run --rm --cidfile=/tmp/remove-cidfile-test quay.io/fedora/fedora true && ls -la /tmp/remove-cidfile-test
-rw-r--r-- 1 rgeissler dev_air 64 Jul 31 20:56 /tmp/remove-cidfile-test
```

It seems that for docker folks it's by design: https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/1761 (only zero length cidfile are clean'ed up). For me it doesn't make sense to leak cidfile when `--rm` was provided.

I guess Podman is better then Docker :^)

The reason I asked is because they should have the same issue with --remote --detatched, since there is noting running on the client side to remove the file.
